learning_outcomes.description: Describe biomechanical principles and how they relate specifically to the analysis of human movement
Perform biomechanical calculations to quantify human movement performance
Conduct a biomechanical analysis of human movement using a variety of instruments
Analyse, interpret and communicate biomechanical data for both performance enhancement and injury prevention in health and disease.
Work effectively within a team in order to develop appropriate biomechanical analysis and communication skills, and facilitate peer-assisted, collaborative learning.

(In-person) Week 2 3/08/2026 - 7/08/2026

(In-person) Week 6 31/08/2026 - 4/09/2026

(In-person) Week 8 14/09/2026 - 18/09/2026

Completed during timetabled practical.
assessments.task_description:
There are 5 practical sessions. The first practical is conducted online and must be completed in Week 1. The following 4 practicals require in-person attendance and active participation to complete the associated practical task.

Use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment requires students to apply biomechanical concepts and methods to the analysis of human movement. Working collaboratively in groups, students will collect, process, analyse and interpret biomechanical data from a movement task. The assessment is designed to develop skills in movement analysis, critical interpretation of biomechanical evidence, and communication of findings to a relevant audience.

Groups will be required to identify the purpose of their analysis and select appropriate biomechanical variables, principles and methods to address their chosen movement question. Using digital tools and biomechanical analysis techniques, students will collect and evaluate movement data, interpret the findings in relation to biomechanical theory and relevant evidence, and consider the implications for performance, injury prevention, rehabilitation, training or movement optimisation.

The outcomes of the project will be communicated through a group video presentation. The presentation should demonstrate the group's ability to:

  • define a clear aim and rationale for the biomechanical analysis;
  • collect and analyse movement data using appropriate biomechanical methods and technologies;
  • apply anatomical and biomechanical principles to interpret findings;
  • communicate key outcomes using appropriate visualisations and supporting evidence;
  • provide informed recommendations, practical applications, or future directions based on the analysis.

Students are expected to work collaboratively throughout the project using university-supported digital collaboration tools.

Use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) 

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their video submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: To be submitted via the relevant submission portal on the course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
